example.question: Based on this graph, show the business segments tied to Redwood Retail, capped at 6 results.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment LIMIT 6

example.question: In this graph, name up to 6 business segments that sit under Redwood Retail.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment LIMIT 6

example.question: Which offerings belong to the Payments Network segment at Lattice Finance?
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:OFFERS]->(o:Offering)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND o.company_name = c.name AND s.name IN $segments AND c.name IN $companies
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment, o.name AS offering ORDER BY company, segment, offering

WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND o.company_name = c.name AND s.name IN $segments AND c.name IN $companies
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment, o.name AS offering ORDER BY company, segment, offering

From what we have here, name the business segments at companies with presence in United Kingdom that serve developers.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

In this graph, identify up to 2 companies with offerings under Cloud Platform that use recurring subscriptions.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(:BusinessSegment)-[:OFFERS]->(root:Offering)
MATCH (root)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(o:Offering)
WHERE root.name IN $offerings AND root.company_name = company.name AND o.company_name = company.name
MATCH (o)-[:MONETIZES_VIA]->(:RevenueModel {name: $revenue_model})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company ORDER BY company LIMIT 2

example.question: Based on this graph, name the top 3 channels by segment usage for segments at companies operating in EMEA.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(channel:Channel)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
RETURN channel.name AS channel, COUNT(DISTINCT [company.name, s.name]) AS segment_count
ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(channel:Channel)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
RETURN channel.name AS channel, COUNT(DISTINCT [company.name, s.name]) AS segment_count
ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

example.question: From what we have here, list the offerings in the Public Sector Solutions segment at Aurora Systems.

WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND o.company_name = c.name AND s.name IN $segments AND c.name IN $companies
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment, o.name AS offering ORDER BY company, segment, offering

example.question: Name the business segments that monetize via service fees.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s)-[:OFFERS]->(root_revenue_1:Offering)
WHERE root_revenue_1.company_name = c.name
MATCH (root_revenue_1)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(revenue_offering_1:Offering)-[:MONETIZES_VIA]->(:RevenueModel {name: $revenue_model})
WHERE revenue_offering_1.company_name = c.name
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

example.question: In this graph, identify companies that can serve developers and financial services firms across the company.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s1:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s1.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s1)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_1})
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s2:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s2.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s2)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_2})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company ORDER BY company
